title: Province of Ontario - Canada.ca
content:
The Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) administers the following programs for the Province of Ontario:
- Ontario trillium benefit Ontario energy and property tax credit Northern Ontario energy credit Ontario sales tax credit
- Ontario energy and property tax credit
- Northern Ontario energy credit
- Ontario sales tax credit
- Ontario child benefit (related to the Canada child benefit)
- Ontario senior homeowners' property tax grant

## Ontario trillium benefit (OTB)
The Ontario trillium benefit (OTB) is the combined payment of the Ontario energy and property tax credit , the Northern Ontario energy credit , and the Ontario sales tax credit . The annual OTB entitlement is usually divided by 12 and the payments issued monthly. Your 2026 OTB payments, which are based on your 2025 income tax and benefit return, will be issued on the 10th of each month, starting in July 2026 (see note for exceptions).
The OTB program is legislated and funded by the Province of Ontario. The CRA administers this program on behalf of the province.
If the 10 th of the month falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, the OTB payment is issued on the last working day before the 10 th .
If, on your 2025 return, you chose to wait to get your 2026 OTB entitlement (see question 9: Can I choose to get my OTB in one payment this year rather than monthly? ) and your annual entitlement is over $500, it will be issued in one payment at the end of the benefit year on June 10, 2027 .
If your 2026 OTB annual entitlement is $500 or less, it will be issued in one lump-sum payment in the first payment month (usually July).
If your 2025 return is assessed after June 19, 2026 , see question 5: When is the 2026 OTB issued? for when you can expect to receive your payment.

### Questions and answers
For more information and to see if you should apply, see the Questions and answers .
- Child and family benefits calculator
If you have a tax policy question about the OTB program, call the Province of Ontario at 1‑866‑ONT‑TAXS (1-866-668-8297) .

## Ontario energy and property tax credit (OEPTC)
If you would like to calculate your entitlement, please go to one of the two following links:
- Ontario energy and property tax credit calculation sheets and print and complete the applicable sheet; or
- Child and family benefits calculator and enter the requested information.
The Ontario energy and property tax credit (OEPTC) is designed to help low- to moderate-income Ontario residents with the sales tax on energy and with property taxes.
You apply for the current-year OEPTC on your prior-year income tax and benefit return. For example, you would apply for the 2026 OEPTC on your 2025 income tax and benefit return by completing Form ON-BEN, Application for the 2026 Ontario Trillium Benefit and the Ontario Senior Homeowners' Property Tax Grant .
The OEPTC has two components: an energy component and a property tax component. You should apply for the OEPTC if you are eligible for either component. You may be eligible if you lived in Ontario on December 31, 2025, and at least one of the following applies:
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Ontario was paid by or for you for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025;
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; or
- you lived in a designated university, college, or private school residence in Ontario in 2025.
Your 2026 OEPTC payments will be issued as part of the OTB , usually on the 10th of each month, starting in July 2026, unless the following exceptions apply:
- If the 10 th of the month falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, the OTB payment is issued on the last working day before the 10 th .
- If, on your 2025 return, you chose to wait to get your 2026 OTB entitlement and your annual entitlement is over $500, it will be issued in one payment at the end of the benefit year on June 10, 2027 . See question 9: Can I choose to get my OTB in one payment this year rather than monthly? under OTB for more details.
- If your 2026 OTB annual entitlement is $500 or less, it will be issued in one lump-sum payment in the first payment month (usually July).
- If your 2025 return is assessed after June 19, 2026, see question 7: When will I get my 2026 OEPTC payments? , for when you can expect to receive your payment.
- If, on December 31, 2025, you were 64 years of age or older and you owned and occupied a principal residence in Ontario, for which you or someone on your behalf paid property tax for 2025, you may also be eligible for the 2026 Ontario senior homeowners' property tax grant .
- You may also be eligible for the 2026 Northern Ontario energy credit (NOEC) if you resided in Northern Ontario on December 31, 2025, and you (or someone on your behalf) paid: rent or property tax for your principal residence in Northern Ontario for 2025; accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Northern Ontario for 2025; or home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Northern Ontario for 2025.
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Northern Ontario for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Northern Ontario for 2025; or
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Northern Ontario for 2025.

### Questions and answers
For more information about the 2026 OEPTC and to see if you are eligible and should apply, please see OEPTC Questions and answers .
- Child and family benefits calculator
If you have a tax policy question about the OEPTC program, call the Province of Ontario at 1-866-ONT-TAXS (1-866-668-8297).

## Northern Ontario energy credit (NOEC)
If you would like to calculate your entitlement, please go to one of the two following links:
- Northern Ontario energy credit calculation sheets and print and complete the applicable sheet; or
- Child and family benefits calculator and enter the requested information.
The Northern Ontario energy credit (NOEC) is designed to help low- to moderate-income Northern Ontario residents with the higher energy costs they face living in the north.
You apply for the current-year NOEC on your prior-year income tax and benefit return. For example, you would apply for the 2026 NOEC on your 2025 income tax and benefit return by completing Form ON-BEN, Application for the 2026 Ontario Trillium Benefit and the Ontario Senior Homeowners' Property Tax Grant .
You may be eligible if you lived in Northern Ontario on December 31, 2025 , and you (or someone on your behalf) paid:
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Northern Ontario for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Northern Ontario for 2025; or
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Northern Ontario for 2025.
Your 2026 NOEC payments will be issued as part of the OTB on the 10th of each month, starting in July 2026, unless the following exceptions apply:
- If the 10 th of the month falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, the OTB payment is issued on the last working day before the 10 th .
If the 10 th of the month falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, the OTB payment is issued on the last working day before the 10 th .
- If, on your 2025 return, you chose to wait to get your 2026 OTB entitlement and your annual entitlement is over $500, it will be issued in one payment at the end of the benefit year on June 10, 2027 . See question 9: Can I choose to get my OTB in one payment this year rather than monthly? under OTB for more details.
If, on your 2025 return, you chose to wait to get your 2026 OTB entitlement and your annual entitlement is over $500, it will be issued in one payment at the end of the benefit year on June 10, 2027 . See question 9: Can I choose to get my OTB in one payment this year rather than monthly? under OTB for more details.
- If your 2026 OTB annual entitlement is $500 or less, it will be issued in one lump-sum payment in the first payment month (usually July).
If your 2026 OTB annual entitlement is $500 or less, it will be issued in one lump-sum payment in the first payment month (usually July).
- If your 2025 return is assessed after June 19, 2026 , see question 8: When will I get my 2026 NOEC payments? , for when you can expect to receive your monthly or lump sum payment.
- If, on December 31, 2025 , you were 64 years of age or older and you owned and occupied a principal residence in Ontario, for which you or someone on your behalf paid property tax for 2025, you may also be eligible for the 2026 Ontario senior homeowners' property tax grant .
- You may also be eligible for the 2026 Ontario energy and property tax credit (OEPTC) if you lived in Ontario on December 31, 2025, and at least one of the following applies: rent or property tax for your principal residence in Ontario was paid by or for you for 2025; accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; or you lived in a designated university, college, or private school residence in Ontario in 2025.
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Ontario was paid by or for you for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025;
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; or
- you lived in a designated university, college, or private school residence in Ontario in 2025.

### Questions and answers
For more information about the 2026 NOEC and to see if you are eligible and should apply, please see NOEC Questions and answers .
- Child and family benefits calculator
If you have a tax policy question about the NOEC program, call the Province of Ontario at 1-866-ONT-TAXS (1-866-668-8297).

## Ontario sales tax credit (OSTC)
The Ontario sales tax credit (OSTC) is a tax-free payment designed to provide relief to low- to moderate-income Ontario residents for the sales tax they pay.
For payments based on your 2025 income tax and benefit return (July 2026 to June 2027), the program provides a maximum annual credit of $378 for each adult and each child in a family. If you are a single individual with no children, the credit will be reduced by 4% of your adjusted net income over $29,047. If you are a single parent, or are married or living in a common-law relationship, the credit will be reduced by 4% of your adjusted family net income over $36,309. These payments are being issued as part of the OTB on the 10th of each month, starting in July 2026. See note for more details and exceptions.
For payments based on your 2024 income tax and benefit return (July 2025 to June 2026), the program provides a maximum annual credit of $371 for each adult and each child in a family. If you are a single individual with no children, the credit will be reduced by 4% of your adjusted net income over $28,506. If you are a single parent, or are married or living in a common-law relationship, the credit will be reduced by 4% of your adjusted family net income over $35,632. These payments will be issued as part of the OTB, usually on the 10th of each month.
For the 2020, 2023, 2024 and 2025 tax years, your family OSTC amount could be calculated using a seniors’ income threshold if you, or your spouse or common-law partner, are a senior (64 years old or older on December 31 st of the applicable tax year). See the Ontario Sales Tax Credit – Seniors’ Income Threshold Questions and Answers for more details.
You do not need to apply for the OSTC. The CRA will use the information from your income tax and benefit return to determine your eligibility. We will send you a notice if you are entitled to receive the credit.
- If the 10 th of the month falls on a weekend or statutory holiday, the OTB payment is issued on the last working day before the 10 th .
- Your OSTC entitlement will be added to your Ontario energy and property tax credit and/or the Northern Ontario energy credit , if applicable, and you will receive a combined payment, called the Ontario trillium benefit (OTB). The annual OTB entitlement is usually divided by 12 and payments are issued monthly (unless notes 3 or 4 below apply).
- If, on your 2025 return, you chose to wait to get your 2026 OTB entitlement and your annual entitlement is over $500, it will be issued in one payment at the end of the benefit year on June 10, 2027. See question 9: Can I choose to get my OTB in one payment this year rather than monthly? under OTB for more details.
- If your 2026 OTB annual entitlement is $500 or less, it will be issued in one lump-sum payment in the first payment month (usually July).
- If you meet the eligibility requirements and your 2025 return is assessed by June 19, 2026, your 2026 OSTC payments will be issued on the 10th of each month, as part of your OTB payment, starting in July 2026 (unless notes 3 or 4 apply).
- If you meet the eligibility requirements and your 2025 return is assessed after June 19, 2026, your first OTB payment, which will include any prior month entitlements, will be issued within four to eight weeks after your 2025 return is assessed. Your remaining payments will be issued on the 10th of each month thereafter (unless notes 3 or 4 apply).
- Booklet RC4210 – Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit
- Child and family benefits calculator
If you have a tax policy question about the NOEC program, call the Province of Ontario at 1‑866-ONT-TAXS (1-866-668-8297).

## Ontario child benefit (OCB)
The Ontario child benefit (OCB) is a tax-free amount paid to help low- to moderate-income families provide for their children.
OCB payments are delivered with the Canada child benefit in a single monthly payment. For July 2026 to June 2027, you may be eligible to receive up to $146.66 per month for each child under 18 years of age. If your adjusted family net income is above $26,865, you may receive a partial benefit.
The OCB program is funded entirely by the Province of Ontario. The CRA administers this program for Ontario.

### Other related information
- Booklet T4114 – Canada Child Benefits
- Canada child benefit - How we calculate your CCB
- Child and family benefits calculator
For more information, go to Ministry of Children, Community and Social Services or call 1-888-789-4199 . For teletypewriter service (TTY), call 1-800-387-5559 .

## Ontario senior homeowners' property tax grant (OSHPTG)
The Ontario senior homeowners' property tax grant (OSHPTG) is intended to help offset property taxes for seniors who own their own home and who have low to moderate incomes. This program is funded entirely by the Province of Ontario. The CRA administers this program for Ontario.
Senior homeowners can apply for the current-year OSHPTG when they file their prior-year income tax and benefit return. For example, you would apply for the 2026 grant on your 2025 income tax and benefit return.
The OSHPTG payment will be issued approximately four to eight weeks after your notice of assessment or reassessment is issued.
The maximum grant is $500 for 2010 and later years.
- You may also be eligible for the 2026 Ontario energy and property tax credit (OEPTC) if you lived in Ontario on December 31, 2025, and at least one of the following applies: rent or property tax for your principal residence in Ontario was paid by or for you for 2025; accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025; you lived in a designated university, college, or private school residence in Ontario in 2025.
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Ontario was paid by or for you for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025;
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Ontario were paid by or for you for 2025;
- you lived in a designated university, college, or private school residence in Ontario in 2025.
- You may also be eligible for the 2026 Northern Ontario energy credit (NOEC) if you resided in Northern Ontario on December 31, 2025, and you (or someone on your behalf) paid: rent or property tax for your principal residence in Northern Ontario for 2025; accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Northern Ontario for 2025; or home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Northern Ontario for 2025.
- rent or property tax for your principal residence in Northern Ontario for 2025;
- accommodation costs for living in a public or non-profit long-term care home in Northern Ontario for 2025; or
- home energy costs (for example, electricity, heat) for your principal residence on a reserve in Northern Ontario for 2025.

### Questions and answers
To find out if you are eligible to apply and to get more information, please see OSHPTG Questions and answers .
- Child and family benefits calculator
If you have a tax policy question about the OSHPTG program, call the Province of Ontario at 1-866-ONT-TAXS (1-866-668-8297).
